types.name: Framework
types.description: An interpretive stance over primitives. Carries affirms and denies functions (Primitive -> Bool) and a consistency proof that no primitive is both affirmed and denied. A framework may be silent on a primitive. Admissible frameworks may contradict each other; each is only required to be internally consistent.

types.name: Ontology
types.description: A finite list of primitives, representing any ontology amenable to neutral-substrate analysis. Not restricted to neutral content; causal and normative primitives are valid members. The predicates (containsCausalOrNormative, Neutral, ExtensionStable, InterpretivelyNonCommitted) determine whether a given Ontology satisfies neutrality conditions. Implemented as a transparent type alias (abbrev) for List Primitive to preserve computability for native_decide and List.any.

types.name: Primitive
types.description: The basic unit of ontological commitment. Carries a kind (PrimitiveKind) and an id (String). DecidableEq enables computational equality checks; Repr enables printing for debugging.

types.name: Primitive kind
types.description: The three mutually exclusive kinds a primitive may have: causal (asserts a causal relation), normative (asserts a normative conclusion), or neutral (asserts only existence or identity). The trichotomy is exhaustive. Every primitive is classifiable as exactly one of these.
